content:
Q1 began with strong momentum as the new onboarding flow shipped in January. Activation rates rose twelve percent month over month and support ticket volume for setup issues fell sharply.

In February the team turned to reliability. We eliminated the top three causes of API timeouts and introduced a status page. Enterprise customers specifically called out the improved transparency during the two brief incidents that month.

March was dominated by the pricing refresh. The new Team and Business tiers launched mid-month, and while conversion dipped briefly during the transition, expansion revenue from existing accounts more than compensated by quarter end.

Looking ahead to Q2, the roadmap centers on the mobile experience. Dark mode, offline drafts, and push notifications are the three most requested features and are now scheduled. We also plan to double the search rate limit after repeated customer requests.

Risks for the quarter include the migration of the billing system, which touches every paid account, and the hiring ramp on the platform team, which is currently behind plan. Both are being tracked weekly by the leadership group.
